title: Money word problems (U.S.)
description: Practice solving situational problems involving money, including adding and subtract money values.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jamir has the following money in his pocket:

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/17ffbb3dbff5355c91460602273b6694.png)

Then, he found $3$ nickels, $2$ dimes, and $4$ pennies in his toy box.

**How much money does Jamir have in all?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

First, let's find how much money Jamir has in his pocket.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/17ffbb3dbff5355c91460602273b6694.png)

$\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}} + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} 
 + \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}= 47¢$

Jamir has $47¢$ in his pocket.

Now, let's add the amount of money Jamir found in his toy box.

$\underbrace{47¢}_{{\text{pocket}}} +\underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{3}{\text{ nickels}}} + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{2}{\text{ dimes}}} +  \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{4}{\text{ pennies}}} = 86¢$

Jamir has $86¢$ between in his pocket and his toy box.
Jamir has $86¢$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cam was cleaning his living room. He found the following coins tucked in the couch cushions:

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4277652c3b76aa7e2df6aad0a8dfbb0d.png)

Then, he found $2$ quarters, $3$ dimes, and $1$ penny under the rug.

**How much money did Cam find in all?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

First, let's find how much money Cam found in his couch.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4277652c3b76aa7e2df6aad0a8dfbb0d.png)

$\underbrace{\goldE{5¢} +\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{3}{\text{ nickels}}} 
 + \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}= 17¢$

Cam found $17¢$ in his couch.

Now, let's add the amount of money Cam found under the rug.

$\underbrace{17¢}_{{\text{couch coins}}} +\underbrace{\blueE{25¢} + \blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}} + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{3}{\text{ dimes}}} +  \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{1}{\text{ penny}}} = 98¢$

Cam found $98¢$ while cleaning his living room.
Cam found $98¢$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Olga has $1$ dime, $2$ nickels, and $3$ pennies in her purse.

Her Oma gave her the following coins from her purse:

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82329e34a734dcca14c8333018558934.png)

**How much money does Olga have in all? **  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

First, let's find how much money Olga had in her purse.

$\underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}} +  \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} + \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}+ \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{3}{\text{ pennies}}} = 23¢$

Olga had $23¢$ in her purse.

Now, let's add the amount of money Olga's Oma gave her.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82329e34a734dcca14c8333018558934.png)

$\underbrace{23¢}_{{\text{Olga's purse}}} +\underbrace{\blueE{25¢} + \blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{3}{\text{ nickels}}} +  \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}+ \maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{4}{\text{ pennies}}} = 92¢$

Olga had $92¢$ between her purse and the money her Oma gave her.
Olga has $92¢$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ari had this much money in her piggy bank before her birthday.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/026509802b11807daa9891014d66bd30.png)

On her birthday, she got $2$ one dollar bills and $1$ ten dollar bill.

**How much money does Ari have now?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Which bill matches the image?

Let's find out how much money Ari has in her piggy bank.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/026509802b11807daa9891014d66bd30.png)

$\underbrace{\$\maroonD{10} + \$\maroonD{10}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ ten dollar bills}}}+\underbrace{\$\purpleD{5}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ five dollar bill}}} + \underbrace{\$\blueE{1}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ one dollar bill}}}= \$26$

Ari has $\$26$ in her piggy bank.

On her birthday, she got $2$ one dollar bills and $1$ ten dollar bill. Let's add the money to the $\$26$ in her piggy bank.

$\underbrace{\$26}_{{\text{ piggy bank money}}} + \underbrace{\$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ one dollar bills}}}  + \underbrace{\$\maroonD{10}}_{\maroonD{1}{\text{ ten dollar bill}}}= \$38$

Ari had $\$38$ after her birthday.
Ari has $\$38$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Zang has $2$ one dollar bills, $1$ five dollar bill, and $3$ ten dollar bills.  

After mowing lawns, he earned this much money:

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/936e323cbf2d71e6c2a74c19d7216453.png)

**How much money does Zang have in all? **  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Which bill matches the image?

Let's find out how much money Zang has.

$\underbrace{\$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ one dollar bills}}}+\underbrace{\$\purpleD{5}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ five dollar bill}}} + \underbrace{\$\maroonD{10} + \$\maroonD{10} + \$\maroonD{10}}_{\maroonD{3}{\text{ ten dollar bills}}}= \$37$

Zang has $\$37$.

Now, let's add the amount of money Zang earned mowing lawns:


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/936e323cbf2d71e6c2a74c19d7216453.png)


$\underbrace{\$37}_{{\text{Zang's money}}} + \underbrace{\$\maroonD{10}}_{\maroonD{1}{\text{ ten dollar bill}}} + \underbrace{\$\purpleD{5}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ five dollar bill}}} + \underbrace{\$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1}}_{\blueE{4}{\text{ one dollar bills}}} = \$56$

Zang has $\$56$ after he mowed lawns.
Zang has $\$56$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mandy was paid the following amount of money for babysitting over the weekend. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8ff7b272ca849fead7117918ef1c1145.png)

Mandy spent $\$18$ on a new pair of shoes.

**How much money does Mandy have left?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Which bill matches the image?

Let's find out how much money Mandy earned for babysitting.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8ff7b272ca849fead7117918ef1c1145.png)

$\underbrace{\$\goldE{20} + \$\goldE{20}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ twenty dollar bills}}} + \underbrace{\$\blueE{1} + \$\blueE{1}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ one dollar bills}}}= \$42$

Mandy earned $\$42$ babysitting.

Now, let's subtract the amount of money she used to buy her new pair of shoes.

$\underbrace{\$42}_{\text{babysitting money}} - \underbrace{\$18}_{\text{money spent on shoes}} = \$24$
Mandy had $\$24$ left.

Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\blueE{45}$ pennies

Penny $ =1¢$, so $\blueE{45}$ pennies $= \blueE{45}¢$

###$\maroonD1$ quarter

Quarter $ =\maroonD{25}¢$

###$\goldE5$ dimes

Dime $ =10¢$, so $\goldE5$ dimes $=\goldE{50}¢$

$\goldE{50}¢ > \blueE{45}¢ > \maroonD{25}¢$

$\goldE5$ dimes $>\blueE{45}$ pennies $>\maroonD1$ quarter
$\goldE5$ dimes is worth the most amount of money.

Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\blueE{8}$ pennies

Penny $ =1¢$, so $\blueE{8}$ pennies $= \blueE{8}¢$

###$\maroonD2$ quarters

Quarter $ ={25}¢$, so $\maroonD{2}$ quarters $= \maroonD{50}¢$

###$\purpleD6$ nickels

Nickel $ ={5}¢$, so $\purpleD{6}$ nickels $= \purpleD{30}¢$

$\maroonD{50}¢ > \purpleD{30}¢ > \blueE{8}¢$

$\maroonD2$ quarters $>\purpleD{6}$ nickels $>\blueE8$ pennies
$\maroonD2$ quarters is worth the most amount of money.

Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\blueE{42}$ pennies

Penny $ =1¢$, so $\blueE{42}$ pennies $= \blueE{42}¢$

###$\goldE3$ dimes

Dime $ =10¢$, so $\goldE3$ dimes $=\goldE{30}¢$

###$\purpleD{9}$ nickels

Nickel $ ={5}¢$, so $\purpleD{9}$ nickels $= \purpleD{45}¢$

$\purpleD{45}¢ > \blueE{42}¢ > \goldE{30}¢$

$\purpleD9$ nickels$>\blueE{42}$ pennies $>\goldE3$ dimes
$\purpleD9$ nickels is worth the most amount of money.

Every bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\blueE{32}$ one dollar bills

One dollar bill $= \$1$, so $\blueE{32}$ one dollar bills $= \$\blueE{32}$

###$\maroonD3$ five dollar bills

Five dollar bill $ =\${5}$, so $\maroonD3$ five dollar bills $ = \$\maroonD{15}$

###$\goldE2$ twenty dollar bills

Twenty dollar bill $=\${20}$, so $\goldE2$ twenty dollar bills $= \$\goldE{40}$

$\$\goldE{40} > \$\blueE{32} >  \$\maroonD{15}$

$\goldE2$ twenty dollar bills $ > \blueE{32}$ one dollar bills $>\maroonD3$ five dollar bills
$\goldE2$ twenty dollar bills  is worth the most amount of money.

Every bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\maroonD{10}$ five dollar bills

Five dollar bill $= \$5$, so $\maroonD{10}$ five dollar bills $= \$\maroonD{50}$

###$\goldE3$ twenty dollar bills

Twenty dollar bill $=\${20}$, so $\goldE3$ twenty dollar bills $= \$\goldE{60}$

###$\purpleD7$ ten dollar bills

Ten dollar bill $=\${10}$, so $\purpleD7$ ten dollar bills $= \$\purpleD{70}$

$\$\purpleD{70} > \$\goldE{60} >  \$\maroonD{50}$

$\purpleD7$ ten dollar bills $ > \goldE{3}$ twenty dollar bills $>\maroonD{10}$ five dollar bills
$\purpleD7$ ten dollar bills  is worth the most amount of money.

Every bill has a different value.

Dollar name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
One dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf614d8a30f58b9d99c3f482b9c8d11.png) | $\$1$
Five dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/509d4d695254f232bbdf6db085ee1249.png) | $\$5$
Ten dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b1225b8c5541ce5887aa333be1b46a2.png) | $\$10$
Twenty dollar bill | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76bff9f86d9f6570bd8e5b00326a42ba.png) | $\$20$

Let's find the value for each amount of money.

###$\maroonD{9}$ five dollar bills

Five dollar bill $= \$5$, so $\maroonD{9}$ five dollar bills $= \$\maroonD{45}$

###$\purpleD4$ ten dollar bills

Ten dollar bill $=\${10}$, so $\purpleD4$ ten dollar bills $= \$\purpleD{40}$

###$\blueE{48}$ one dollar bills

One dollar bill $= \$1$, so $\blueE{48}$ one dollar bills $= \$\blueE{48}$

$\$\blueE{48} > \$\maroonD{45} >  \$\purpleD{40}$

$\blueE{48}$ one dollar bills $ > \maroonD{9}$ five dollar bills $>\purpleD{4}$ ten dollar bills
$\blueE{48}$ one dollar bills  is worth the most amount of money.
